details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

function.content:
---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.family_map_wall_board
alias: family_map_wall_board
purchased_on: 2026-03-02
total_eur: 36
counterparty: Möbelhaus Malmö AB
project: Family Map Wall
related_entity: Ida Novak
lines:
  - item: pin board
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 29
    line_eur: 29
  - item: colored map pins
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 7
    line_eur: 7
---
# Map wall board and pin set

```text
+----------------+----------------------------+
| field          | value                      |
+----------------+----------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                       |
| bill_id        | bill.family_map_wall_board |
| alias          | family_map_wall_board      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-03-02                 |
| total_eur      | 36                         |
| counterparty   | Möbelhaus Malmö AB         |
| project        | Family Map Wall            |
| related_entity | Ida Novak                  |
+----------------+----------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item             | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | pin board        | 1   | 29       | 29       |
| 2 | colored map pins | 1   | 7        | 7        |
+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL            |     |          | 36       |
+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

One of those warm household purchases that only becomes part of the story once the map wall gets named.

next_step.current_state: First bill OCR migration write succeeded. Continue migrating remaining Ida-related bills with required frontmatter while preserving exact original bodies.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Delete processed inbox file and report completion
next_step.observation: Successfully wrote frontmatter to 2026_03_02__eur_000036__bill__family_map_wall_board.md.

function.message:
Processed the next inbox request and OCR-migrated all bills related to Ida Novak by adding bill-schema YAML frontmatter while preserving each original note body verbatim. Updated files:
- 50_finance/purchases/2026_03_02__eur_000036__bill__family_map_wall_board.md
- 50_finance/purchases/2026_03_24__eur_000022__bill__toy_forge_wheel_and_magnet_pack.md
- 50_finance/purchases/2026_03_31__eur_000025__bill__school_helper_label_tape.md
Then deleted the processed inbox file 00_inbox/000_next-task.md.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed identity bootstrap (tree, root AGENTS, context), read 99_system and workflow docs before task work, used canonical entity record for Ida resolution, applied finance schema-defined bill fields, preserved original note bodies during frontmatter migration, and deleted inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: Inbox request explicitly asked to OCR all bills related to Ida Novak. Canonical entity record (10_entities/cast/ida.md) resolved Ida identity. Three purchase records explicitly marked related_entity as Ida Novak and each contained complete visible bill + line-item data required by finance schema. All three were updated in place with required bill frontmatter fields and unchanged body content, satisfying OCR/migration workflow constraints.
completed_steps_laconic: write frontmatter to three bill files
